questions.q: この英文の内容として最も適切なものを1つ選びなさい。
questions.a: 日本の会社員は残業代はきちんともらえるが、休暇はほとんど取らずに働き続けている。
questions.b: 日本の会社員には休暇を取る法的な権利がなく、そのため一切休むことができない。
questions.c: 日本の会社員の残業は無給のことが多く、法律で認められた休暇も全部は取らないことが多い。
questions.d: 日本の会社員は休暇をすべて消化し残業も少ないので、働きすぎとは言えない。
questions.explanation: 本文は残業について『しばしば無給(often unpaid)』、休暇について『法的権利(legal right)のある分を全部取ることはめったにない(rarely take the full amount)』と述べる。この2点を正しく言い換えたCが正解。Aは残業代が支払われるとする点でoften unpaidと矛盾。Bは休暇の権利がないとする点でlegal rightと正反対。Dは残業が少なく休暇も全消化とする点が、work a lot of extra hours / rarely take full vacation と真逆で誤り。

questions.q: Which one has the closest meaning to the passage above?
questions.a: Japanese workers have a reputation around the world for working too much; much of their overtime goes unpaid, and they seldom use all the holidays that the law allows them.
questions.b: Japanese workers are famous worldwide for working too much, and although all of their overtime is paid, they take almost no vacation at all.
questions.c: Japanese workers are known for working too much and for taking long holidays, and they are usually paid well for the extra hours they put in.
questions.d: Japanese workers rarely work extra hours, but when they do the hours are unpaid, and they use up every day of holiday they are legally given.
questions.explanation: The passage says Japanese employees are known for overworking, that their extra hours are often unpaid, and that they rarely take all the vacation they are legally entitled to — all restated in A. B is wrong because it says the overtime is fully paid; C wrongly claims they take long holidays and are paid well for overtime; D reverses the meaning by saying they seldom work extra hours yet use up all their holiday.
